testPage.urlNote: The URL in latest.json was /blog/test-post-hero/ which returns 404. The correct working URL is /test-post-hero/ (without /blog/ prefix). Permalink structure may need flushing.
variationDescriptions: Default - Page Accent Color (neon-green gradient, no excerpt)
With Excerpt Shown (neon-green gradient, excerpt visible below title)
Topic Accent Color Override (blue gradient from topic accent, no excerpt)
Full Options - Excerpt + Topic Color (blue gradient, excerpt visible)

observations: Overall shape and proportions match Figma design
Full-width hero section with background image fading via mask gradient matches Figma pattern
Content is left-aligned in the left portion of the viewport, consistent with Figma
Topic pill, title, author/date meta stack vertically in correct order
Gradient overlay transitions from image at top to accent-colored gradient at bottom
Visual weight and density match the Figma reference
Full-width section with relative positioning -- matches Figma
hero-inner container with background image layer + content layer structure -- matches Hero secondary pattern
Content constrained to max-w-[700px] mr-auto (left-aligned, max 700px) -- matches Figma content width of 719px outer / 687px inner
Container utility provides correct horizontal centering: at 1440px viewport, container side margin = 64px, matching Figma content start at x=64
Top padding uses calc(var(--header-height)+280px) at sm+ which accounts for header overlap plus content offset
Background image fills full section with w-full h-full object-cover
Mask gradient (black 40% to transparent 100%) fades image progressively from top to bottom -- matches Figma
Both -webkit-mask-image and mask-image are applied for cross-browser support
Image renders correctly in Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit -- mask gradient works in all browsers
top-gradient div present and renders the accent-colored gradient overlay
Default variation uses page accent color (neon-green) -- gradient appears as yellow-green blend matching Figma
useTopicColor variations correctly switch to blue gradient from topic accent color
Gradient extends below the hero section appropriately via h-[150vw] and -z-10
Pill renders with rounded-full shape, visible border, light background -- matches Figma pill proportions
Figma: p-[8px] (8px all around). Implementation: px-2 py-2 (8px all around) -- EXACT MATCH
Figma: 14px Roboto Medium uppercase, leading 1.1. Implementation: text-sm font-medium uppercase leading-[1.1] -- EXACT MATCH
Figma: bg #eeffb5 (accent-lighter), border #ddff6a (accent). Implementation: bg-accent-lighter border-accent -- tokens map correctly
Pill text is charcoal (#1f1f1d) via text-charcoal -- EXACT MATCH
Topic accent color class scoped to pill element correctly colors the pill independently of the section gradient
Figma: Anton Regular 64px, line-height 1.1, charcoal
Implementation: text-header-0 (Anton Regular 64px at desktop), text-charcoal
Title renders in large bold-looking Anton font (NOT italic -- correct, Anton appears slanted by design)
Line-height: implementation uses 1.2 (text-header-0 default) vs Figma 1.1. Difference is 0.1 at 64px = ~6.4px total on multi-line text. Accepted as within tolerance per planning spec decision DEC-003
Correctly hidden in variations 1 and 3 (showExcerpt=false)
Correctly visible in variations 2 and 4 (showExcerpt=true)
Uses text-body-large (18px Roboto) text-charcoal mt-6 (24px gap from title area)
Text renders at appropriate size and weight, readable against the gradient background
Figma: Roboto Medium 18px, line-height 1.2, charcoal. Implementation: text-lg font-medium leading-[1.2] text-charcoal -- EXACT MATCH
Format 'By: {name}' and 'Published on: {date}' matches Figma text
Meta group positioned with mt-12 (48px) below upper group -- matches Figma gap of 48px (243 - 195 = 48)
Pill-to-title gap: Figma 24px, implementation gap-6 (24px) -- EXACT MATCH
Upper-to-lower group gap: Figma 48px, implementation mt-12 (48px) -- EXACT MATCH
Bottom padding desktop: Figma 64px, implementation sm:pb-16 (64px) -- EXACT MATCH
Bottom padding mobile: implementation pb-8 (32px) -- appropriate for mobile
All text uses text-charcoal (#1f1f1d) -- matches Figma charcoal color
Topic pill uses accent color system (bg-accent-lighter, border-accent) scoped via category accent class
Gradient uses accent color system via .hero class and .top-gradient CSS
No hardcoded hex values found in implementation -- all use theme tokens

375px (mobile): Content stacks vertically, title wraps naturally, all elements visible and readable. Pill, title, meta all left-aligned. Background image and mask gradient work correctly. Smaller bottom padding (pb-8 = 32px).
768px (tablet): Layout scales smoothly between mobile and desktop. Content constrained to max-width, gradient and image render correctly. Larger bottom padding (sm:pb-16 = 64px) applies.
1024px (desktop): Full desktop layout, content left-aligned with max-width constraint. All elements proportionally correct.
1440px (desktop): Matches Figma reference. Full-width hero with correct proportions.
Chromium: Renders correctly at all breakpoints
Firefox: Renders identically to Chromium -- mask-image gradient works correctly
WebKit: Renders identically -- -webkit-mask-image fallback works correctly
No cross-browser inconsistencies detected
Variation 1 (Default): Page accent color gradient (neon-green), no excerpt -- renders correctly
Variation 2 (With Excerpt): Same gradient, excerpt visible between title and meta -- renders correctly
Variation 3 (Topic Color): Gradient switches to blue (topic accent color) -- demonstrates useTopicColor toggle working correctly
Variation 4 (Full Options): Blue gradient + excerpt visible -- both options work together correctly

issues.recommendation: Accepted per planning decision DEC-003. The text-header-0 utility uses 1.2 line-height by default. Adding a custom leading-[1.1] would override this but diverge from the design system. Difference is within acceptable tolerance.

issues.figmaValue: Single text block with inherent line-height spacing (~3.6px between lines)
issues.recommendation: Implementation uses separate paragraph elements for semantic clarity (allows independent translatable prefixes). The visual gap (4px vs ~3.6px) is imperceptible. No change needed.
